item.title: How the Supreme Court Came to Accept a Practice It Called Unjust
item.description: The Court went from condemning partisan gerrymandering to effectively encouraging it.
item.dc:creator: David A. Graham

item.title: Europe Without America
item.description: The Iran war has given European leaders new impetus to plan for self-defense.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Stanley-Becker

item.title: The Candidate From ICE
item.description: A GOP primary in Ohio will test Trump’s mass-deportation push.
item.dc:creator: Nick Miroff

item.title: Why Stocks Keep Going Up
item.description: The boom is not as untethered from reality as it may look.
item.dc:creator: James Surowiecki

item.title: The End of Cigarettes Is Coming
item.description: The U.K. is phasing out smoking. How long will Americans tolerate tobacco—and other vices?
item.dc:creator: Charles Fain Lehman

item.title: More Photos From Artemis II
item.description: NASA just released more than 12,000 images from their recent 10-day trip around the moon and back, collecting photographs taken by the four astronauts aboard and automated systems on the Orion spacecraft.
item.dc:creator: Alan Taylor

item.title: Why One Coach’s Personal Life Is a Sports-Wide Scandal
item.description: Mike Vrabel is facing questions about his personal relationship with a reporter. He should answer them.
item.dc:creator: Jemele Hill

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: Aquatic Geography
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: The One Tax the Rich Can’t Escape
item.description: New York’s proposed pied-à-terre tax is unlikely to chase anyone away.
item.dc:creator: Richard Florida

item.title: How Everest Has Changed SinceInto Thin Air
item.description: Scaling the world’s highest mountain is a very different experience than it was when I climbed it.
item.dc:creator: Jon Krakauer

item.title: The Venture-Capital Populist
item.description: How David Sacks and the new tech right went full MAGA and captured Washington
item.dc:creator: George Packer

item.title: The Only Thing Worse Than Spirit Airlines
item.description: … is a world without Spirit Airlines.
item.dc:creator: Saahil Desai

item.title: A HilariousSNLSketch About Heartbreak Goggles
item.description: It shrewdly illustrated all the ways that breakups can distort reality.
item.dc:creator: Paula Mejía

item.title: Reflections in the Door of a School Bus and Other Doors
item.dc:creator: Athena Nassar

item.title: The Cost of ‘Natural’ Womanhood
item.description: Hormone hype is out of control.
item.dc:creator: Andréa Becker

item.title: The Rise of Emotional Surveillance
item.description: Companies are monitoring workers not just for productivity but for agreeability.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Cushing

item.title: DOJ Enters a New, Even More Aggressive Phase
item.description: The department is growing bolder yet, cutting legal corners in service of getting President Trump the headlines—and revenge—he wants.
item.dc:creator: Quinta Jurecic

item.title: A Great Actor Who Is Also a Perfect Celebrity
item.description: Culture and entertainment recommendations from Dan Zak
item.dc:creator: Dan Zak

item.title: The Era of Rational Discourse Is Over
item.description: For Jürgen Habermas, who died in March, the essence of democracy was thoughtful back-and-forth argument.
item.dc:creator: Adam Kirsch

item.title: How the Iran War May Play a Role in the Midterms
item.description: Panelists joined to discuss the economic fallout from the conflict, and more.
item.dc:creator: The Editors
